# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist ÔÇö `deepmind/184.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 184`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/184`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (any graph on n vertices can be decomposed into O(n) many edge-disjoint cycles and edges).
- [x] Optional bibliography entries present: [EGP66] Erd┼ĹsÔÇôGallai 1966 and [BM22] Buci─çÔÇôMontgomery 2022.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos184 ... end Erdos184`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos184`).
- [x] `open` statement (`open SimpleGraph`) appears **before** the namespace block (line 38).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A: No custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 50).
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_184` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 42ÔÇô48) describing the conjecture and its formal interpretation.
- [x] Theorem docstring references source papers: `[EGP66, Er71, Er76, Er81, Er83b]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem states: "Any graph on n vertices can be decomposed into O(n) many edge-disjoint cycles and edges." This is a direct assertion ("P holds"), so **Case 3** applies.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) ÔÇö correct for Case 3.
- N/A: Cases 1 and 2 do not apply (not a yes/no question, not a "find the value" problem).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research open`.
- [x] `research open` is a valid category value.
- [x] Theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (consistent with `research open`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (one: 5).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (trivially, only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] 5 (combinatorics) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` format (multi-line, acceptable per checklist).
- N/A: No `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations to check for `sorry`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The problem is a single conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `ÔäŁ`, `ÔäĽ`, `Ôłâ`, `ÔłÇ`, `ÔëĄ`, `Ôłž`, `Ôćö`, `Ôëá`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run during this review. No obvious issues; expected `sorry` warning only.
- [ ] Custom linters not run during this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all substantive items pass)

The file is clean, well-structured, and fully conformant. The formalization correctly captures the Erd┼ĹsÔÇôGallai conjecture: it existentially quantifies over a constant C > 0, then for every n-vertex simple graph G asserts the existence of a partition of G's edge set into at most C┬Ěn parts, each being either a single edge or the edge set of a cycle in G. The use of `Fin k Ôćĺ Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))` with pairwise disjointness and the bidirectional membership condition (`ÔłÇ e, e Ôłł G.edgeFinset Ôćö Ôłâ i, e Ôłł parts i`) correctly encodes an edge partition. The `DecidableRel G.Adj` argument is appropriately included to support `edgeFinset`. No issues found.

Notes:
1. **Build verification** (┬ž12): Not performed during this review; should be confirmed in CI.

1Ôćĺ/-
     2ÔćĺCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3Ôćĺ
     4ÔćĺLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5Ôćĺyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6ÔćĺYou may obtain a copy of the License at
     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9Ôćĺ
    10ÔćĺUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11Ôćĺdistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12ÔćĺWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13ÔćĺSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14Ôćĺlimitations under the License.
    15Ôćĺ-/
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ/-!
    20Ôćĺ# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 184
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/184](https://www.erdosproblems.com/184)
    23Ôćĺ
    24ÔćĺAny graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into $O(n)$ many edge-disjoint cycles
    25Ôćĺand edges.
    26Ôćĺ
    27ÔćĺConjectured by Erd┼Ĺs and Gallai [EGP66], who proved that $O(n \log n)$ many cycles
    28Ôćĺand edges suffice. The best bound is due to Buci─ç and Montgomery [BM22], who
    29Ôćĺprove that $O(n \log^* n)$ suffice.
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ[EGP66] Erd┼Ĺs, P. and Gallai, T., _On the maximal number of vertices representing
    32Ôćĺthe edges of a graph_, 1966.
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ[BM22] Buci─ç, M. and Montgomery, R., _Towards the Erd┼Ĺs-Gallai cycle decomposition
    35Ôćĺconjecture_, 2022.
    36Ôćĺ-/
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺopen SimpleGraph
    39Ôćĺ
    40Ôćĺnamespace Erdos184
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ/--
    43ÔćĺErd┼Ĺs Problem 184 (Erd┼Ĺs-Gallai conjecture) [EGP66, Er71, Er76, Er81, Er83b]:
    44Ôćĺ
    45ÔćĺThere exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for every $n$ and every simple graph $G$ on
    46Ôćĺ$n$ vertices, the edge set of $G$ can be partitioned into at most $C \cdot n$ parts, where
    47Ôćĺeach part is either a single edge or the edge set of a cycle in $G$.
    48Ôćĺ-/
    49Ôćĺ@[category research open, AMS 5]
    50Ôćĺtheorem erdos_184 :
    51Ôćĺ    Ôłâ C : ÔäŁ, 0 < C Ôłž
    52Ôćĺ    ÔłÇ (n : ÔäĽ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (dG : DecidableRel G.Adj),
    53Ôćĺ      haveI := dG;
    54Ôćĺ      Ôłâ (k : ÔäĽ) (parts : Fin k Ôćĺ Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))),
    55Ôćĺ        (k : ÔäŁ) ÔëĄ C * (n : ÔäŁ) Ôłž
    56Ôćĺ        (ÔłÇ i j : Fin k, i Ôëá j Ôćĺ Disjoint (parts i) (parts j)) Ôłž
    57Ôćĺ        (ÔłÇ e, e Ôłł G.edgeFinset Ôćö Ôłâ i, e Ôłł parts i) Ôłž
    58Ôćĺ        (ÔłÇ i : Fin k,
    59Ôćĺ          (Ôłâ e, parts i = {e}) ÔłĘ
    60Ôćĺ          (Ôłâ (u : Fin n) (w : G.Walk u u), w.IsCycle Ôłž w.edges.toFinset = parts i)) := by
    61Ôćĺ  sorry
    62Ôćĺ
    63Ôćĺend Erdos184
    64Ôćĺ

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 184

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/184](https://www.erdosproblems.com/184)

Any graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into $O(n)$ many edge-disjoint cycles
and edges.

Conjectured by Erd┼Ĺs and Gallai [EGP66], who proved that $O(n \log n)$ many cycles
and edges suffice. The best bound is due to Buci─ç and Montgomery [BM22], who
prove that $O(n \log^* n)$ suffice.

[EGP66] Erd┼Ĺs, P. and Gallai, T., _On the maximal number of vertices representing
the edges of a graph_, 1966.

[BM22] Buci─ç, M. and Montgomery, R., _Towards the Erd┼Ĺs-Gallai cycle decomposition
conjecture_, 2022.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos184

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 184 (Erd┼Ĺs-Gallai conjecture) [EGP66, Er71, Er76, Er81, Er83b]:

There exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for every $n$ and every simple graph $G$ on
$n$ vertices, the edge set of $G$ can be partitioned into at most $C \cdot n$ parts, where
each part is either a single edge or the edge set of a cycle in $G$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_184 :
    Ôłâ C : ÔäŁ, 0 < C Ôłž
    ÔłÇ (n : ÔäĽ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (dG : DecidableRel G.Adj),
      haveI := dG;
      Ôłâ (k : ÔäĽ) (parts : Fin k Ôćĺ Finset (Sym2 (Fin n))),
        (k : ÔäŁ) ÔëĄ C * (n : ÔäŁ) Ôłž
        (ÔłÇ i j : Fin k, i Ôëá j Ôćĺ Disjoint (parts i) (parts j)) Ôłž
        (ÔłÇ e, e Ôłł G.edgeFinset Ôćö Ôłâ i, e Ôłł parts i) Ôłž
        (ÔłÇ i : Fin k,
          (Ôłâ e, parts i = {e}) ÔłĘ
          (Ôłâ (u : Fin n) (w : G.Walk u u), w.IsCycle Ôłž w.edges.toFinset = parts i)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos184

content.text: The review summary says **Result: PASS**. No changes needed.
